requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'Nexus: Proactive Intra-GPU Disaggregation of Prefill and Decode in LLM Serving' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The infrastructure, models, and datasets required for the Nexus system have been set up.
The required hardware environment is available.
A workstation with at least one NVIDIA L20 GPU (48GB) is available; for multi-GPU experiments, two NVIDIA L20 GPUs are available.

verification.spec: Run `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv,noheader` and verify at least one GPU reports 'NVIDIA L20' with ~48GB memory; for multi-GPU tests, verify at least two L20 GPUs are listed.

sub_tasks.requirements: The software environment uses CUDA 12.8 with driver 570.124.04 and PyTorch 2.6.0.

verification.spec: Run `nvidia-smi` to confirm driver version 570.124.04, `nvcc --version` to confirm CUDA 12.8, and `python -c 'import torch; print(torch.__version__)'` to confirm PyTorch 2.6.0.

sub_tasks.requirements: NVIDIA MPS is available for SM partitioning control in motivational experiments.

verification.spec: Verify NVIDIA MPS is available by running `nvidia-cuda-mps-control -d` followed by `echo get_default_active_thread_percentage | nvidia-cuda-mps-control`; expect a numeric percentage returned without error.

sub_tasks.requirements: The LLM models used in evaluations are available.
The Qwen2.5-3B model weights are available for use.

verification.spec: Load the Qwen2.5-3B model via `from transformers import AutoConfig; AutoConfig.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B')` and confirm it loads without error; check that hidden_size and num_hidden_layers match Qwen2.5-3B specifications.

sub_tasks.requirements: The LLaMA3.1-8B model weights are available for use.

verification.spec: Load the LLaMA3.1-8B model via `from transformers import AutoConfig; AutoConfig.from_pretrained('meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B')` and confirm it loads without error.

sub_tasks.requirements: The Qwen2.5-14B model weights are available for use in dual-GPU experiments.

verification.spec: Load the Qwen2.5-14B model via `from transformers import AutoConfig; AutoConfig.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B')` and confirm it loads without error.

sub_tasks.requirements: Workload datasets have been prepared for evaluation.
The ArXiv Summarization dataset is available (mean input ~3832 tokens, mean output ~200 tokens).

verification.spec: Load the ArXiv Summarization dataset (paper-abstract pairs). Tokenize inputs and outputs with the Qwen2.5-3B tokenizer and confirm mean input length ~3832 tokens (within 10%) and mean output length ~200 tokens (within 15%).

sub_tasks.requirements: The ShareGPT dataset is available (mean input ~496 tokens, mean output ~97 tokens).

verification.spec: Load the ShareGPT dataset. Tokenize inputs and outputs with a HuggingFace tokenizer (e.g., LLaMA3.1-8B) and confirm mean input length ~496 tokens (within 15%) and mean output length ~97 tokens (within 20%).

sub_tasks.requirements: The Mixed Workload is constructed as 60% ShareGPT and 40% Long Data Collections requests.

verification.spec: Invoke the Mixed Workload construction routine to sample N=1000 requests. Tag each request with its source dataset, then assert that the fraction from ShareGPT is between 0.55 and 0.65 and the fraction from Long Data Collections is between 0.35 and 0.45.

sub_tasks.requirements: Request arrival times for all workloads are generated using a Poisson process at configurable request rates.

verification.spec: Call the arrival-time generator with a target rate lambda (e.g., 2.5 req/s) and N>=2000 samples. Compute inter-arrival times and (1) verify their mean is within 5% of 1/lambda, and (2) perform a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test against an exponential distribution with rate lambda; assert p-value > 0.05.

sub_tasks.requirements: The Nexus system has been implemented as an extension of vLLM v1-0.8.1.
Concurrent prefill and decode execution within a single serving engine has been implemented.
Prefill and decode phases are launched as separate coroutines, each managing its own GPU stream and scheduler.

verification.spec: Inspect Nexus source code to verify prefill and decode are launched as separate asyncio coroutines (or equivalent), each holding distinct CUDA stream handles and distinct scheduler objects. Grep for `asyncio.create_task` or `coroutine` near the engine main loop and confirm two streams via `torch.cuda.Stream()` instantiated for prefill and decode paths.

sub_tasks.requirements: The main loop coordinates execution between the prefill and decode coroutines and handles shared metadata updates safely.

verification.spec: Inspect the Nexus engine source (modified vLLM main loop). Verify that a single asyncio/main loop awaits/coordinates both the prefill and decode coroutines, and that shared metadata structures (e.g., KV cache manager, request table) are updated through the main loop or guarded helpers rather than independently by each coroutine.

sub_tasks.requirements: Critical shared state is guarded to prevent inconsistencies during overlapping execution since both phases share worker threads.

verification.spec: Grep the Nexus source for synchronization primitives (threading.Lock, asyncio.Lock, mutex, atomic, etc.) protecting shared state accessed by both prefill and decode paths (e.g., KV cache table, request queue, partition state). Assert at least one lock/guard is present around the shared metadata update sites identified in the worker code.

sub_tasks.requirements: Prefill and decode queues are maintained independently with pluggable phase-specific scheduling logic, extending vLLM's unified scheduler.

verification.spec: Inspect the scheduler module. Verify two distinct queue objects exist (one for prefill, one for decode) and that the scheduler exposes a pluggable interface (e.g., a base class with a 'schedule' method or strategy parameter) allowing swap-in of SPF/FCFS policies per phase.

sub_tasks.requirements: Dynamic SM partitioning using CUDA Green Contexts has been implemented.
A PyTorch extension (approximately 150 lines of CUDA code) exposes CUDA Green Context APIs to Python.

verification.spec: Locate the PyTorch CUDA extension exposing Green Context APIs (e.g., a setup.py or pyproject defining a cpp_extension with .cu/.cpp sources around ~150 lines). Import the extension from Python and verify it exposes functions to create/switch green contexts (e.g., `create_green_ctx`, `set_green_ctx`).

sub_tasks.requirements: All partition layouts are pre-instantiated during initialization to avoid runtime reconfiguration overhead.

verification.spec: Inspect engine initialization. Verify that during startup, the code pre-creates Green Context partition layouts for all candidate SM ratios (e.g., a dict/list of pre-instantiated contexts) before serving begins, and that runtime switching only selects among these pre-built layouts rather than constructing new ones.

sub_tasks.requirements: SM groups are dynamically reassigned at runtime based on the partitioning algorithm's decisions.

verification.spec: Run Nexus on a mixed prefill+decode workload for ~60s with logging of SM ratio decisions. Verify that the SM ratio changes at least 3 times during the run, and that each change is triggered by the partitioning algorithm (i.e., logged decision precedes the GPU context switch within the same iteration).

sub_tasks.requirements: The analytical cost model for predicting per-phase latency has been implemented.
Phase latency is modeled as a sum of operator-level latencies for both prefill and decode operations.

verification.spec: Inspect the cost model implementation. Verify that T_prefill is computed as a sum over an iterable PrefillOps (e.g., KQV, attention, attn_linear, FFN) and T_decode as a sum over DecodeOps, each operator contributing its own latency term — not a single closed-form expression for the whole phase.

sub_tasks.requirements: Per-operator R_sat and lambda parameters are extracted via offline profiling of prefill and decode under varying SM allocations for each model and workload configuration.

verification.spec: Run the offline profiling script for one (model, workload) configuration: it should sweep SM ratios (e.g., 30%-100% in 10% steps), measure prefill and decode latency per operator/stage, fit the two-regime (saturation R_sat + decay lambda) model, and emit a per-operator parameter file. Verify the output file exists and contains R_sat and lambda values for each operator in PrefillOps and DecodeOps.

sub_tasks.requirements: The probability of decode overlapping with memory-bound prefill attention is computed as P_attn = T_prefill^attn / T_prefill.

verification.spec: Inject controlled T_prefill and T_prefill_attn values into the cost model and confirm the computed P_attn equals T_prefill_attn / T_prefill within floating-point tolerance for several test cases (e.g., (T_prefill=10, T_attn=4) -> P_attn=0.4).

sub_tasks.requirements: Effective memory bandwidth for decode is computed as a function of memory bytes accessed by decode attention (m_d), prefill attention (m_p1), and prefill dense operators (m_p2), accounting for contention via P_attn and (1-P_attn).

verification.spec: Feed test inputs (m_d, m_p1, m_p2, P_attn) to the effective-bandwidth function and verify (a) increasing m_p1 or m_p2 decreases the effective bandwidth for decode, (b) the formula combines a P_attn-weighted attention contention term with a (1-P_attn)-weighted dense contention term, matching Equation in §4.1.1.

sub_tasks.requirements: Memory access volume for linear layers is estimated using known parameter sizes from the model architecture.

verification.spec: Inspect the memory-access estimation code for linear layers. Verify it reads layer weight shapes from the model config/architecture (hidden_size d, intermediate_size d_ff, num_layers) and computes bytes accessed = (#params * dtype_size) rather than measuring at runtime.

sub_tasks.requirements: Memory access volume for attention is calculated based on cached tokens tracked by vLLM multiplied by per-token key/value size determined from model dimensions.

verification.spec: Inspect attention memory-access estimation. Verify it queries vLLM's KV cache manager for the number of cached tokens per request, then multiplies by per-token K+V bytes computed from model dims (num_kv_heads * head_dim * 2 * dtype_size * num_layers).

sub_tasks.requirements: Memory contention is modeled only for the decode attention operator, while other decode components assume no contention.

verification.spec: Inspect the decode memory-latency model. Verify that the contention term (effective bandwidth reduction from prefill overlap) is applied only to the decode attention operator's latency computation, and that non-attention decode operators use a non-contended bandwidth model.

sub_tasks.requirements: The dual-objective latency optimization with runtime mode switching has been implemented.
The prefill-priority mode minimizes T_prefill(R_p) subject to T_decode(R_d) <= beta * T_decode^min.

verification.spec: Force optimization mode = prefill-priority and run AdjustPartition with mocked CostModel returning monotonically known latencies. Verify the returned R_p minimizes T_prefill while T_decode(1-R_p) <= beta * T_decode^min (beta=1.3). Sweep R_p from 0 to 100 to confirm the chosen R_p is the largest feasible one.

sub_tasks.requirements: The decode-priority mode minimizes T_decode(R_d) subject to T_prefill(R_p) <= alpha * T_prefill^min.

verification.spec: Force optimization mode = decode-priority and run AdjustPartition with a mocked CostModel. Verify the returned R_d minimizes T_decode while T_prefill(1-R_d) <= alpha * T_prefill^min (alpha=1.1). Sweep R_d to confirm the chosen value is the largest feasible one.

sub_tasks.requirements: The mode switches based on KV cache usage KV_u: prefill-priority when KV_u is low, decode-priority when KV_u exceeds a threshold (set to 70% of available KV cache memory).

verification.spec: Set KV cache usage to 50% and check the optimization objective selects prefill-priority mode; raise KV_u to 75% (above 70% threshold) and verify the mode switches to decode-priority. Inspect the mode-selection function output for several KV_u values straddling the threshold.

sub_tasks.requirements: Slack parameters are set to alpha=1.3 for prefill and beta=1.1 for decode (tight slack for decode given SPF aggressively optimizes TTFT).

verification.spec: Grep the Nexus configuration/source for alpha and beta slack parameters; confirm alpha=1.3 (prefill slack) and beta=1.1 (decode slack) are the default constants.

sub_tasks.requirements: The greedy SM search algorithm (Algorithm 1) has been implemented.
Phase 1 (constraint satisfaction) of the algorithm decreases the SM share of the prioritized stage until the non-prioritized stage's latency constraint is satisfied.

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic scenario where the non-prioritized stage's predicted latency violates its slack bound at the current SM split. Run the greedy partitioner and verify it iteratively decreases the prioritized stage's SM share (R - 1 per step) until CostModel(other, 100-R) <= slack * T_other_opt, matching Algorithm 1 lines 21-23.

sub_tasks.requirements: Phase 2 (target optimization) of the algorithm gradually increases the SM share of the prioritized stage while keeping the constraint satisfied.

verification.spec: Start the greedy partitioner from a feasible allocation and verify it increases R by 1 each step, stopping as soon as CostModel(other, 100-(R+1)) > slack * T_other_opt, matching Algorithm 1 lines 24-30.

sub_tasks.requirements: The search converges within approximately 2-4 cost model evaluations per update.

verification.spec: Instrument AdjustPartition to count CostModel invocations per call. Run Nexus on the Mixed workload for >=200 partition updates and report the mean number of CostModel evaluations per update. Compare to paper claim of 2-4.

sub_tasks.requirements: Hysteresis-based asynchronous SM repartitioning for stability has been implemented.
The runtime controller tracks the last-applied SM ratio and only triggers repartitioning when the new target differs by more than threshold delta.

verification.spec: Set last-applied SM ratio to R_old. Submit candidate new ratios that differ by less than delta and confirm the controller does NOT trigger a repartition (no change to active green context). Submit a candidate differing by more than delta and confirm a repartition is triggered.

sub_tasks.requirements: SM partition switching is asynchronous: streams switch independently without global synchronization points.

verification.spec: Inspect the SM-switching mechanism. Verify there is no global barrier/sync call between prefill and decode streams at partition change time — instead, each phase's stream applies its new Green Context independently when scheduled, and no cudaDeviceSynchronize / event-wait coordinates them at the switch.

sub_tasks.requirements: Phase-specific schedulers have been implemented.
The Shortest-Prompt-First (SPF) scheduler for prefill (Algorithm 2) computes per-request score as r.remaining - gamma * r.age.

verification.spec: Construct a queue with requests of varying prompt_len, prefilled_len, and age. Invoke the SPF scheduler and verify computed scores equal (prompt_len - prefilled_len) - gamma * age for each request, and that requests are sorted ascending by this score before greedy batching.

sub_tasks.requirements: SPF sorts requests by score and greedily adds to batch until the token budget B is exceeded.

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic queue of requests with known remaining prompt lengths and arrival times. Call SPF_Schedule with budget B. Verify (1) requests are processed in ascending score order (score = remaining - gamma*age), and (2) the function stops appending as soon as adding the next request would push cumulative remaining tokens above B.

sub_tasks.requirements: The default anti-starvation factor gamma in SPF is set to 15.

verification.spec: Grep the SPF scheduler source/config for the default gamma value and confirm it equals 15.

sub_tasks.requirements: The decode scheduler uses a First-Come-First-Serve (FCFS) policy.

verification.spec: Submit a sequence of decode requests with distinct arrival timestamps to Nexus's decode scheduler and verify they are dispatched in arrival-time order (FCFS) until the batch budget is filled.

sub_tasks.requirements: Both schedulers use vLLM's default chunk size for prefill and default maximum batch size.

verification.spec: Inspect Nexus config loading. Verify the prefill chunk size (max_num_batched_tokens) and max batch size (max_num_seqs) match vLLM v1-0.8.1's default values when no override is provided. Print the loaded values and compare against vLLM defaults.

sub_tasks.requirements: Baseline serving systems have been set up for comparison.
vLLM v1.0.8.1 with FCFS scheduling, continuous batching, PagedAttention, and chunked prefill is available as a baseline.

verification.spec: Install vLLM v1.0.8.1 and verify importability (vllm.__version__ == '0.8.1'). Launch the engine with FCFS scheduling policy, enable_chunked_prefill=True, and continuous batching; confirm PagedAttention is the default attention backend. Issue a single test request and confirm a completion is returned.

sub_tasks.requirements: FastServe (v0.0.8.1) with multi-level feedback queue and skip-join is reimplemented atop vLLM with CPU swap (120GB per device) and recomputation fallback enabled.

verification.spec: Verify the FastServe reimplementation exists atop vLLM v0.0.8.1: (1) scheduler module implements a multi-level feedback queue with skip-join policy; (2) engine config exposes CPU swap of 120 GB per device; (3) a recomputation fallback path is invoked when swap is exhausted. Run a smoke test issuing 4 requests and confirm completions return.

sub_tasks.requirements: SGLang v0.4.4.post1 (commit 3c09548) with RadixAttention, chunked prefill, PagedAttention, and FCFS scheduling is available as a baseline.

verification.spec: Install SGLang v0.4.4.post1 at commit 3c09548 and verify importability. Launch with --enable-chunked-prefill, FCFS scheduling, RadixAttention enabled (default), and PagedAttention backend. Issue a test request and confirm a completion is returned.

sub_tasks.requirements: vLLM-P/D (v1.0.8.5) with prefill-decode disaggregation via LMCache is available, configured with one prefill instance and one decode instance on separate GPUs.

verification.spec: Install vLLM v1.0.8.5 with LMCache integration. Launch one prefill instance on GPU 0 and one decode instance on GPU 1, with KV transfer routed through LMCache. Send one test request, confirm prefill executes on GPU 0, decode on GPU 1, and the completion is returned.

sub_tasks.requirements: Evaluation infrastructure and metrics have been implemented.
Time-to-first-token (TTFT) is measured as the delay before the first output token is produced.

verification.spec: Submit a request with a known prompt, record the wall-clock time at submission and the wall-clock time of the first emitted output token; verify TTFT equals (first_token_time - submission_time).

sub_tasks.requirements: Time-between-tokens (TBT) is measured as the latency between consecutive output tokens.

verification.spec: For a single request, record timestamps of every emitted token and compute inter-token latencies; verify TBT corresponds to the time gap between consecutive decode tokens (excluding the first token).

sub_tasks.requirements: Normalized Latency is computed as end-to-end latency divided by the number of output tokens.

verification.spec: Generate N requests, for each request compute (end_time - start_time) / num_output_tokens; verify the reported Normalized Latency equals this per-request value (and aggregated mean/P95 match).

sub_tasks.requirements: Both mean and 95th-percentile values are reported for TTFT, TBT, and Normalized Latency.

verification.spec: Inspect the evaluation script outputs / generated tables and verify that for each metric (TTFT, TBT, Normalized Latency) both mean and 95th-percentile (P95) values are computed and reported per arrival rate.

sub_tasks.requirements: Maximum sustainable throughput is computed as the highest arrival rate without violating token latency constraints.

verification.spec: Run a sweep across increasing Poisson arrival rates; for each rate measure per-token latency SLO compliance; report the highest arrival rate at which the latency constraint is not violated as the maximum sustainable throughput.

sub_tasks.requirements: Makespan is measured for offline inference experiments as end-to-end time to process all batched requests.

verification.spec: Submit the full set of offline requests at t=0 to a serving system, record the wall-clock time at which the last request completes, and report makespan = last_completion_time - submission_time.

sub_tasks.requirements: Latency breakdown is decomposed into scheduling, queuing (waiting), and execution stages.

verification.spec: Instrument each system to log per-request timestamps for: arrival, scheduling decision, queue exit (execution start), and execution end. Compute scheduling = scheduled - arrival, waiting = exec_start - scheduled, execution = exec_end - exec_start; verify total decomposes into these three stages as in Figure 12.

sub_tasks.requirements: The motivational experiments and observations in Section 3 have been reproduced.
Section 3.1: Latency impact of mixed prefill-decode batches is measured by categorizing batches into prefill-only, decode-only, and mixed and measuring iteration times.
Mixed batches show ~250 ms average iteration time vs ~15 ms for decode-only batches with similar token counts, an 8-10x slowdown.

verification.spec: Run Qwen2.5-3B on a single NVIDIA L20 GPU under the Long Data Collections workload with Poisson arrival rate 2.5 req/s using vLLM with chunked prefill. Instrument the engine to categorize each forward-pass batch as prefill-only, decode-only, or mixed, and log per-iteration wall-clock latency. Compute the average iteration time for mixed batches and for decode-only batches; verify mixed avg ~250 ms vs decode-only ~15 ms, producing a slowdown ratio of approximately 8-10x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Per-kernel profiling shows linear kernel latency in mixed batches is up to 10x higher than in decode-only batches.

verification.spec: Run Qwen2.5-3B on a single L20 GPU with vLLM chunked prefill on Long Data Collections at 2.5 req/s. Use Nsight Systems or torch.profiler to capture per-kernel runtimes (specifically the linear/GEMM kernels: KQV projection, attention output projection, FFN linear). Compute the average linear-kernel latency in mixed prefill+decode batches vs decode-only batches; verify the ratio is up to ~10x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Section 3.3: Memory contention impact under static partitioning has been measured by co-executing prefill chunks and pure decode under fixed SM partitions.
Increasing prefill KV length from 2000 to 10000 increases the same decode batch's latency by approximately 36%.

verification.spec: On Qwen2.5-3B on a single L20 with a fixed SM partition (matching Section 3.3), co-execute a fixed decode batch with prefill chunks where the prefill request has KV length 2000 vs 10000. Measure mean decode-iteration latency over ~50 iterations and compute the percentage increase between the two settings; expect ~36%.

sub_tasks.requirements: End-to-end single-GPU performance experiments (Section 6.2.1) have been executed.
All five systems have been evaluated on the Arxiv Summarization workload using Qwen2.5-3B.

verification.spec: Run all five baseline systems with Qwen2.5-3B on the ArXiv Summarization workload at multiple Poisson arrival rates; verify each system completes and reports the metrics shown in Figure 9 row 2.

sub_tasks.requirements: All five systems have been evaluated on the Mixed workload using LLaMA3.1-8B.

verification.spec: Run all five baseline systems with LLaMA3.1-8B on the Mixed workload (60% ShareGPT + 40% Long Data Collections) at Poisson arrival rates 1-3 req/s; verify each system completes and the produced metrics correspond to Figure 9 row 3.

sub_tasks.requirements: Single-GPU TBT claims have been verified.
vLLM-P/D achieves the best average and P95 TBT among all systems (due to full disaggregation across GPUs).

verification.spec: Across all three single-GPU workloads, run all five systems and compare mean and P95 TBT; verify vLLM-P/D (using 2 GPUs) achieves the lowest mean and P95 TBT among all systems.

sub_tasks.requirements: Nexus achieves 1.24x to 1.48x lower TBT than vLLM on single-GPU workloads.

verification.spec: Run Nexus and vLLM (v1.0.8.1) on a single NVIDIA L20 GPU with Qwen2.5-3B (Long Data Collections, Arxiv) and Llama3.1-8B (Mixed) under Poisson arrivals matching Figure 9 arrival rates. Measure mean TBT for each system at matching load points. Compute ratio TBT_vLLM / TBT_Nexus and verify it falls within [1.24, 1.48] for at least the Long Data Collections workload at 2.5 req/s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Single-GPU throughput claims have been verified.
Nexus achieves 1.5x to 1.8x higher throughput than vLLM on Long Data Collections and Arxiv.

verification.spec: Determine maximum sustainable throughput (highest arrival rate without SLO violation) for vLLM and Nexus on Long Data Collections (Qwen2.5-3B) and ArXiv (Qwen2.5-3B); verify the ratio Nexus/vLLM is between 1.5x and 1.8x on both workloads.

sub_tasks.requirements: Nexus achieves 1.18x to 1.27x higher throughput than SGLang on Long Data Collections and Arxiv.

verification.spec: Measure maximum sustainable throughput of SGLang and Nexus on Long Data Collections and ArXiv with Qwen2.5-3B; verify Nexus achieves between 1.18x and 1.27x SGLang's throughput on both.

sub_tasks.requirements: On Mixed Workload, Nexus achieves 1.9x higher throughput than vLLM.

verification.spec: On a single L20 GPU using Llama-3.1-8B with the Mixed workload (60% ShareGPT + 40% Long Data Collections, Poisson arrivals), run both vLLM v1.0.8.1 and Nexus. Sweep arrival rates and determine the maximum sustained throughput (highest req/s where mean normalized latency stays below the saturation threshold consistent with Figure 9 row 3). Compute Nexus_max_throughput / vLLM_max_throughput and verify ratio ~1.9x.

sub_tasks.requirements: On Mixed Workload, Nexus achieves 1.8x higher throughput than SGLang.

verification.spec: Measure max sustainable throughput of SGLang and Nexus on the Mixed Workload with LLaMA3.1-8B on single L20; verify Nexus achieves approximately 1.8x SGLang's throughput.

sub_tasks.requirements: On Mixed Workload, Nexus achieves 1.4x higher throughput than vLLM-P/D despite using half the GPUs.

verification.spec: Run Mixed Workload on LLaMA3.1-8B: Nexus on 1 L20 vs vLLM-P/D (one prefill instance + one decode instance) on 2 L20 GPUs. Measure each system's max sustainable throughput and compute Nexus/(vLLM-P/D) ratio; expect ~1.4x in Nexus's favor despite using half the GPUs.

sub_tasks.requirements: End-to-end multi-GPU performance experiments (Section 6.2.2) have been executed.
vLLM, SGLang, Nexus, and vLLM-P/D have all been evaluated on Qwen2.5-14B with the Mixed Workload on two NVIDIA L20 GPUs.

verification.spec: Run vLLM, SGLang, Nexus, and vLLM-P/D each on 2x NVIDIA L20 GPUs (TP=2) serving Qwen2.5-14B under the Mixed Workload across arrival rates up to 2 req/s; verify each system produces valid outputs and reports the metrics shown in Figure 10.

sub_tasks.requirements: Multi-GPU results sweep multiple arrival rates and report both mean and P95 normalized latency, TTFT, and TBT.

verification.spec: Inspect multi-GPU experiment outputs and verify that for at least 3 arrival rates, the script reports mean AND P95 values for each of: normalized latency, TTFT, and TBT (matching the 6 subplots in Figure 10).

sub_tasks.requirements: Nexus achieves 2.2x higher throughput than vLLM on Qwen2.5-14B Mixed Workload using the same hardware.

verification.spec: On two L20 GPUs, run Qwen2.5-14B with the Mixed Workload Poisson arrival benchmark for both Nexus and vLLM. Find each system's maximum sustainable throughput (highest arrival rate without normalized latency exceeding the constraint used in Section 6.2.2). Compute Nexus_throughput / vLLM_throughput; expect ~2.2x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Nexus achieves 2x higher throughput than SGLang on Qwen2.5-14B Mixed Workload.

verification.spec: On two L20 GPUs, run Qwen2.5-14B Mixed Workload benchmark for both Nexus and SGLang v0.4.4.post1. Determine max sustainable throughput for each and compute Nexus/SGLang ratio; expect ~2x.

sub_tasks.requirements: vLLM-P/D performance is shown to be poor due to aggressive prefill saturating the transfer buffer and causing cache eviction/recomputation.

verification.spec: Run vLLM-P/D on Qwen2.5-14B Mixed Workload at high arrival rate (>=2 req/s); collect logs/metrics showing transfer-buffer saturation events, KV cache evictions, and recomputation; verify these events occur and correlate with TTFT/TBT degradation versus Nexus.

sub_tasks.requirements: Offline inference experiments (Section 6.3) under heterogeneous prompts have been executed.
Offline inference experiments submit all requests at once and measure end-to-end makespan.

verification.spec: In the offline experiment driver, submit all N requests simultaneously at t=0 (no Poisson arrival), wait for completion of every request, and report makespan = max(completion_time) - 0; verify no inter-arrival sleeps are inserted.

sub_tasks.requirements: Offline inference is evaluated on Long Data Collections workload at 512, 1024, and 2048 total jobs using Qwen2.5-3B on a single L20.

verification.spec: Run offline inference on a single L20 with Qwen2.5-3B on Long Data Collections, submitting 512, 1024, and 2048 jobs at once for vLLM, SGLang, FastServe, Nexus (and vLLM-P/D on 2 GPUs); verify all systems complete (or time out as reported) and record makespan for each job count.

sub_tasks.requirements: Offline inference is evaluated on Mixed Workload at 1024, 2048, and 4096 total jobs using LLaMA3.1-8B on a single L20.

verification.spec: Run offline inference benchmark on Mixed Workload using LLaMA3.1-8B on a single NVIDIA L20 GPU, submitting all requests at once with batch sizes 1024, 2048, and 4096 total jobs. Confirm experiment harness executes and produces makespan measurements for each job count.

sub_tasks.requirements: Nexus achieves 5-50% lower makespan than vLLM and SGLang on Long Data Collections offline inference.

verification.spec: Run offline inference on single L20 with Qwen2.5-3B using Long Data Collections workload, submitting all requests at once (batch sizes 512, 1024, 2048 as in Figure 11a) for Nexus, vLLM, and SGLang. Measure end-to-end makespan (time from submission until last response). Compute (makespan_baseline - makespan_Nexus)/makespan_baseline for both vLLM and SGLang at each batch size; verify at least one configuration falls within 5-50% reduction.

sub_tasks.requirements: On Mixed Workload offline inference, Nexus outperforms vLLM by ~5% but lags SGLang by 8-15% in makespan.
